title: Secure named partner org (need, priority categories, languages, endorsement)
context: repair-cafe-kits is 'planning stage; no adopting partner secured yet' — every task carries verifiedNeed: false. To meet the full Definition of Shipped, a named partner organization (a library system, repair network, local council/waste authority, or community-sheds federation) must confirm the need in writing, name their priority item categories and target languages, and endorse the kit. Without a partner, the project can still declare 'Publicly Shipped (generic public good)' 6 months after the M3 build is production-ready, but a named partner is the preferred outcome. This task is pursued in parallel from the start of the project.
objective: Identify, approach, and secure a named partner organization that confirms the need for repair-cafe-kits in writing, names priority item categories and target languages, and provides an endorsement (letter of support or MOU); record the outcome and trigger verifiedNeed and requestor updates across tasks.
acceptanceCriteria: A named partner organization is identified and approached from target types: public library system, repair network, local council / waste authority, community / men's sheds federation

The partner confirms need in writing (letter of support or MOU) and specifies: their priority item categories (to inform content-016 and backlog prioritization) and target language(s) (to inform l10n-015)

On success, a committed document records the partner org name, contact, confirmation date, priority categories, and target languages; verifiedNeed is updated to true and requestor is set to the named org across relevant tasks in a follow-up PR

If no partner is secured by 6 months after the M3 production build is ready, the document records the search outcome and the steward + Elyos governance declare 'Publicly Shipped (generic public good)' per the PLAN fallback
The engagement process must not collect any attendee or community-member PII; partner contact information is stored off-platform per standard data-minimization practice
output: A committed document recording the partner outreach outcome: either a named partner's letter of support/MOU with confirmed priority categories and languages, or the search outcome and fallback declaration timeline.
